steps.title: Find the historians' view
steps.content: The passage says some people criticized pachuca style, but historians see it differently. Historians describe the style as "a form of rebellion against the era's rigid social expectations for women" and as showing "a desire for self-expression and freedom."

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content:
• Choice A - They think that pachuca style was similar to other fashion trends that different groups of women adopted in the same period.: The passage does not compare pachuca style with other fashion trends adopted by other groups of women.
  • Choice B - They think that pachuca style was celebrated because it enabled some Mexican American women to show their support for the United States during World War II.: The passage says the style was criticized, not celebrated as patriotic support.
  • Choice C - They think that pachuca style was such a popular trend that it continues to influence fashion in the United States to the present day.: The passage never discusses popularity over time or present-day fashion influence.

steps.title: Match the supported view
steps.content: Choice D - They think that pachuca style was a way for some Mexican American women to express themselves and resist strict social expectations. This matches the historians' view because it combines the passage's ideas of self-expression, freedom, and rebellion against rigid expectations for women. The answer is D.
